original_abstract: Research disclosed since the demonstration of the first examples of primary amine-thiourea organocatalysis in 2006 has shown that primary amine-based thioureas can successfully catalyze a diverse variety of highly enantioselective transformations providing a wide range of versatile organic compounds. Recent remarkable progress with these chiral catalysts is summarized in this review.
humanized_abstract: Recent advancements in primary amine-thiourea catalysis disclosed after the first report of this methodology in 2006 are summarized. A large number of highly enantioselective catalytic reactions leading to different classes of chiral products catalyzed by primary amine-based thioureas are described. These developments demonstrate the versatility of this class of organocatalysts.
